title: 4/30/19 Swam 5,900 Meters, got watch band & roller wheel

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder, V-Log, April 30th.
So, what time is it?
It is 12.22 p.m.
I just got up. I'm totally tired. That's why I slept in.
Yeah, yesterday I swam 5,891 meters in the pool, in a 15-meter pool.
Well, I'm not doing flip turns.
If I tried that, I would run out of breath and I wouldn't be able to do it.
I'm not going to try that anytime soon.
Maybe in like a week or two, I'll try those.
But then that'll bring up my speed.
That took me two and a half hours.
I uploaded it to Strava, but for some reason, I uploaded two other...
That was my third swim. I've done three swims.
The first was 1,300 meters. The second was 14.
And then I skipped a day. And then yesterday I did 59.
But the first two weren't on Strava.
They're on there for a day or something.
And then they just disappear.
So I'm going to go on Strava and see if the one that I posted is there.
It didn't look like it posted at all, but it showed on my feed.
So hopefully they'll figure all that stuff out.
Because being able to share exactly what I do, it motivates me big time.
So, yeah, yesterday I was kind of tired.
The day before yesterday I was tired from an hour-long swim I did the day before.
But I didn't do anything yesterday because...
Well, I mean, I swear, but in the morning I got up and I was doing house organization and house cleaning.
I can't remember if we did the deck with my mom.
That was two days ago, I think.
My brain has not kicked in yet.
I think it'll take about three months to get all the reefer out of my brain.
And until then, my memory is going to be shoddy.
My short-term memory.
But, yeah, yesterday I got up and I was going to...
One of the things I was going to do is try to see if I get my car to work.
I haven't driven my car for like a year.
And I charged my battery with this old charger.
I had a new charger, but I must have given it to somebody.
But, yeah, it barely charged.
It barely started my car, but my car did start.
But then there's this gas leak.
It's leaking like a sieve in the back.
So I turned it off and I figured I'd have to drive it to the mechanic.
I wanted to drive it to the mechanic before I would try and get the emissions done on it.
Because I didn't want to, like, cause a fire in the emissions, you know?
I could get gas all over people.
But my mechanic didn't answer.
Michael Frey over there on Pearl and 47th.
But, so I had my mom driving down there.
Because in the morning, well, actually, the first thing in the morning yesterday,
I wanted to go swimming, but then my iPod wasn't, had, was empty.
So then I got on tech support with Apple.
They helped me figure out how to fill up the iPod.
You have to drag it onto the music folder in the iTunes app and not in its folder on the sidebar.
Um, but by then, the, uh, and I didn't get a single, I didn't get any sleep night before last, like at all.
Not even a single second.
So I was tired, but at nine o'clock came around.
Then I, I rode my bike into the, uh, REI.
Cause I thought I couldn't figure out how to attach my battery in the extra real pack.
Like, it told me what, um, part I needed, but I actually came home.
I was going to get it from Garmin, but I came home and I found it.
Um, and so then I figured that out.
I put that on there.
And then, um, um, that's when I went over to try to start my car.
Yeah.
And then I had my mom drive me down to the mechanic, but, uh, he had gone out of business and there was another mechanic there.
And he said he'd do my car, but then we decided to just go and check out some mechanics over on, uh, on, um, 30th and, uh, Valmont, because that's one block from where I live.
And there's this one mechanic with, you know, big old, huge letters, Volkswagen, Audi, BMW.
So I went in there, but he said he did not do, um, he did not do vans.
And I mentioned this other mechanic who I used last summer, who, um, who used to work.
